evidence: CLI/TUI tool installed globally via npm/bun (bun install -g dev3000), not a web service
No Dockerfile or docker-compose — no containerization support
No HTTP server or port binding; uses terminal UI (Ink/React) and Chrome DevTools Protocol locally
Stores telemetry in ~/.d3k/ on developer's machine — local dev tool only
No self-hosting documentation or server deployment model
From vercel-labs (experimental org), designed as a local AI debugging aid
reason: dev3000 is a local CLI/TUI development debugging tool that captures logs, screenshots, and network events on the developer's machine. It has no server component, no Docker support, no HTTP port, and is not designed for self-hosted deployment. Fundamentally incompatible with LazyCat's containerized app model.
